title: Disease-Modifying Antirheumatic Drugs (DMARDs)
summary: DMARDs هي الأدوية التي تُعدّل مسار الأمراض الالتهابية كالتهاب المفاصل الروماتويدي وتمنع التدمير المفصلي الذي لا تستطيع مضادات الالتهاب والستيرويدات منعه. تنقسم إلى ثلاث فئات: التقليدية (Methotrexate كدواء محوري، وSulfasalazine وHydroxychloroquine وLeflunomide)، والبيولوجية (مثبطات TNF-α وIL-6 وحاصرات T-cell وB-cell)، والموجّهة الاصطناعية (مثبطات JAK-STAT كـTofacitinib وBaricitinib). Methotrexate هو الدواء المحوري الأول في الروماتويد مع ضرورة إعطاء حمض الفوليك لتقليل السمية.

key_drugs.name: Methotrexate (MTX)
key_drugs.note: الدواء المحوري الأول في RA - مرة أسبوعياً - يعمل عبر زيادة Adenosine ومضادة حمض الفوليك

key_drugs.name: Hydroxychloroquine
key_drugs.note: للروماتويد الخفيف وSLE - السمية الرئيسية: تسمم الشبكية

key_drugs.name: Sulfasalazine
key_drugs.note: للروماتويد وأمراض الأمعاء الالتهابية - كبت نقي العظم

key_drugs.name: Leflunomide
key_drugs.note: يثبط تخليق البيريميدين - ممنوع في الحمل

key_drugs.name: Adalimumab / Etanercept
key_drugs.note: يثبط TNF-α - للاستخدام بعد فشل DMARDs التقليدية

key_drugs.name: Tofacitinib / Baricitinib
key_drugs.note: يثبط مسار JAK-STAT داخل الخلية - فموي - مقيّد بسبب مخاوف أمان

mnemonics.title: DMARDs التقليدية: MSHL
mnemonics.body:
M = Methotrexate (المحوري - الأول)
S = Sulfasalazine
H = Hydroxychloroquine
L = Leflunomide
تذكّر: 'MoSHeL' - كأنك تمشل (تمشي ببطء) لأن DMARDs بطيئة المفعول

mnemonics.title: Methotrexate: 'محارب الفولات'
mnemonics.body: MTX = عدو حمض الفوليك. يحارب DHFR ويمنع تنشيط الفولات فيتوقف تصنيع DNA. لهذا نُعطي الفوليك اسيد دائماً مع MTX لحماية المريض من السمية (الفوليك يقلل السمية دون تقليل الفاعلية). وجرعته مرة أسبوعياً فقط - الجرعة اليومية سامة.

mnemonics.title: Hydroxychloroquine والعين
mnemonics.body: تخيّل دواء على شكل نظارة - Hydroxychloroquine ترتبط بصورة العين في ذهنك لأن سميته الرئيسية هي تسمم الشبكية (Retinal Toxicity). كل مريض يأخذه يحتاج فحص عيون دورياً.

mnemonics.title: البيولوجية: TAR-B
mnemonics.body:
T = TNF inhibitors (Adalimumab, Etanercept)
A = Abatacept (T-cell blocker)
R = Rituximab (B-cell depletion)
B = (Tocilizumab) = Blocks IL-6
جميعها تزيد خطر العدوى - لا بد من فحص TB قبل البدء

mnemonics.title: JAK Inhibitors: 'الجاك الفموي'
mnemonics.body: Tofacitinib وBaricitinib = حاصرات JAK - مميزتهما أنهما فمويتان (oral) خلافاً للبيولوجية التي تُحقن. لكن 'الجاك' له مخاوف أمان: خطر الجلطات (Thrombosis)، إعادة تنشيط Herpes Zoster، ارتفاع الدهون.

flashcards.front: Why are DMARDs called 'disease-modifying'?
flashcards.back: Because they slow or halt structural joint destruction, unlike NSAIDs and steroids which only treat symptoms without preventing disease progression

flashcards.front: What is the anchor drug in the treatment of RA?
flashcards.back: Methotrexate (MTX) — the primary foundational drug in RA upon which all other treatment regimens are built

flashcards.front: What is the dose of Methotrexate in RA and why?
flashcards.back: Once weekly only — daily dosing causes high toxicity due to cumulative antifolate effect

flashcards.front: What is the main anti-inflammatory mechanism of Methotrexate?
flashcards.back: Increases adenosine levels (an anti-inflammatory mediator) — the antiproliferative effect is a secondary mechanism

flashcards.front: Why is folic acid given with Methotrexate?
flashcards.back: MTX inhibits DHFR and blocks folate activation — adding folic acid reduces toxicity (GI side effects, mouth ulcers, cytopenias) without reducing efficacy

flashcards.front: What routine monitoring is required for patients on Methotrexate?
flashcards.back: Monthly: CBC (bone marrow suppression) + LFTs (hepatotoxicity) + creatinine (MTX is renally excreted — renal impairment increases toxicity)

flashcards.front: What is the main toxicity of Hydroxychloroquine?
flashcards.back: Retinal toxicity — requires periodic ophthalmologic examination for early detection

flashcards.front: What are the uses of Hydroxychloroquine?
flashcards.back: Mild rheumatoid arthritis, systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE), mild-to-moderate inflammatory diseases

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of Leflunomide?
flashcards.back: Inhibits pyrimidine synthesis, reducing lymphocyte proliferation and inflammation

flashcards.front: What are the contraindications of Leflunomide and Sulfasalazine?
flashcards.back: Both are contraindicated in pregnancy (teratogenic) — Leflunomide has a very long half-life and requires a special washout procedure before conception

flashcards.front: When are biologics used in RA?
flashcards.back: After failure of conventional DMARDs (especially MTX) or in moderate-to-severe disease not responding to conventional treatment

flashcards.front: What are the most important shared adverse effects of all biologics?
flashcards.back: Increased risk of infections — TB and hepatitis screening must be performed before starting any biologic

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of TNF inhibitors (Adalimumab, Etanercept)?
flashcards.back: Directly inhibit tumor necrosis factor (TNF-α) — a key inflammatory mediator in RA

flashcards.front: What is the role of Abatacept in RA treatment?
flashcards.back: Inhibits T-cell co-stimulation (T-cell activation blockade), reducing the immune response in RA

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of action of JAK inhibitors (Tofacitinib, Baricitinib)?
flashcards.back: Inhibit the intracellular JAK-STAT pathway — the pathway used by inflammatory cytokines to act on cells

flashcards.front: What is the advantage of JAK inhibitors over biologics?
flashcards.back: They are oral — whereas biologics are given by injection or intravenous infusion

flashcards.front: What are the safety concerns of JAK inhibitors?
flashcards.back: Increased risk of thrombosis, reactivation of Herpes Zoster and TB, cytopenias, elevated lipids — therefore their use is restricted

flashcards.front: What is the role of steroids in RA treatment compared to DMARDs?
flashcards.back: Steroids serve as 'bridge therapy' — used temporarily for rapid inflammation control while DMARDs take effect (which takes weeks)

flashcards.front: What is the difference between NSAIDs and DMARDs in rheumatoid arthritis?
flashcards.back: NSAIDs only relieve symptoms; DMARDs modify the disease course and slow joint damage.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Methotrexate in rheumatoid arthritis?
flashcards.back: Inhibits dihydrofolate reductase and reduces inflammation; it is the first conventional DMARD.

flashcards.front: What monitoring is required for Methotrexate?
flashcards.back: Liver function tests, complete blood count, and lung function (fibrosis); folic acid is given to reduce toxicity.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Hydroxychloroquine and its risks?
flashcards.back: An antimalarial used in RA and lupus; risk of retinopathy (periodic eye examination required).

flashcards.front: What are the TNF-α inhibitors?
flashcards.back: Etanercept, Infliximab, Adalimumab — biologic immunomodulatory agents.

flashcards.front: What is the main risk of TNF-α inhibitors?
flashcards.back: Reactivation of tuberculosis and opportunistic infections — screen for TB before starting.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Rituximab?
flashcards.back: Anti-CD20 monoclonal antibody that depletes B cells.

flashcards.front: What is the mechanism of Sulfasalazine?
flashcards.back: A conventional anti-inflammatory DMARD used in rheumatoid arthritis and inflammatory bowel disease.

mcqs.q: A 42-year-old woman with rheumatoid arthritis was treated with NSAIDs for two years and developed joint deformities. What is the main lesson from this case?
options: NSAIDs only treat symptoms and do not prevent joint destruction
The dose of NSAIDs should be increased
mcqs.explanation: NSAIDs relieve pain and stiffness but do not prevent joint destruction. DMARDs are the only drugs that modify the disease course and prevent destruction.

mcqs.q: What is the first anchor drug in the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis unless contraindicated?
mcqs.explanation: Methotrexate is the anchor drug in RA. It is used as the foundation for all treatment protocols.

mcqs.q: A patient is on Methotrexate. Why is folic acid given with it?
options: To enhance the efficacy of MTX
To reduce adverse effects without affecting efficacy
mcqs.explanation: MTX inhibits DHFR and blocks folate activation, causing toxicity (GI effects, mouth ulcers, cytopenias). Folic acid reduces these effects without any impact on therapeutic efficacy.

mcqs.q: What is the most important routine test for a patient taking Hydroxychloroquine?
mcqs.explanation: The main toxicity of Hydroxychloroquine is retinal toxicity. Periodic eye examinations are required to detect it before permanent damage occurs.

mcqs.q: What is the mechanism of action of Leflunomide in RA?
options: Inhibits pyrimidine synthesis and reduces lymphocyte proliferation
mcqs.explanation: Leflunomide inhibits the enzyme DHODH, blocking pyrimidine synthesis and reducing lymphocyte proliferation, which weakens the immune response.

mcqs.q: When are biologics used in the treatment of rheumatoid arthritis?
options: After failure of conventional DMARDs such as Methotrexate
mcqs.explanation: Biologics are used after failure of conventional DMARDs (especially MTX) in moderate-to-severe disease. They are second-line agents.

mcqs.q: Which adverse effect is shared by all biologic agents in RA?
mcqs.explanation: All biologic agents increase the risk of infections due to immunosuppression. Therefore, TB and hepatitis B and C screening must be performed before starting treatment.

mcqs.q: What is the advantage of Tofacitinib and Baricitinib over biologic agents?
mcqs.explanation: JAK inhibitors (Tofacitinib, Baricitinib) are oral drugs, whereas most biologics are given by injection or IV infusion — this is their main advantage.

mcqs.q: What is the main role of steroids at the start of RA treatment with DMARDs?
options: They replace DMARDs in mild cases
Temporary bridge therapy until the DMARD takes effect
They prevent the adverse effects of MTX
mcqs.explanation: Steroids are used as bridge therapy for rapid inflammation control while DMARDs take weeks to begin working. They do not replace DMARDs in controlling disease progression.

mcqs.q: A patient is about to start a biologic. What is the mandatory test before starting?
mcqs.explanation: Before starting any biologic or JAK inhibitor, TB and hepatitis (B and C) screening is required because these drugs suppress immunity and may reactivate latent infections.

mcqs.q: Before starting TNF-α inhibitors, the following must be screened:
Folic acid is given with Methotrexate to:
mcqs.explanation: Reduces hepatic and bone marrow toxicity.

high_yield.point: Methotrexate هو الـ DMARD التقليدي الأول؛ يثبّط dihydrofolate reductase.
MTX يحتاج مراقبة: كبد، تعداد دم، رئة (تليف). يُعطى folic acid لتقليل السُّميّة.
bDMARDs مثبطات TNF-α: Etanercept, Infliximab, Adalimumab → خطر إعادة تنشيط السل (افحص قبل البدء).
